paper_title: Synthesis and Evaluation of Ion-Exchange Resins for Cs-137 Removal

methods.method_name: Synthesis of Non-Porous and Porous Ion-Exchange Resins
methods.description: This method describes the synthesis of non-porous and porous ion-exchange resins using resorcinol and formalin, with the addition of pore-forming agents like CaCO3 or toluene to achieve different morphologies.

steps.description: The synthesis of non-porous RFR was carried out through the interaction of an alkaline solution of resorcinol and formalin under the conditions shown in Table 5.
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize non-porous RFR using resorcinol and formalin

steps.description: The solidification of the ion-exchange resin was performed in air atmosphere at 210 °C for 6 h.
steps.searchable_summary: solidify ion-exchange resin at 210 °C for 6 hours

steps.description: The resulting ion-exchange resins were ground and transformed into the H+ form through sequential washing by HNO3 (0.5 mol/L)-NaOH (0.5 mol/L)-HNO3 (0.5 mol/L) solutions under dynamic conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: transform ion-exchange resins into H+ form using sequential washing

steps.description: The porous morphology of the ion-exchange resins was achieved using CaCO3 or toluene as pore-forming agents.
steps.searchable_summary: create porous morphology using CaCO3 or toluene

methods.description: This method evaluates the efficiency of Cs-137 removal using ion-exchange resins under static and dynamic conditions, including the determination of distribution coefficients and kinetic parameters.

steps.description: A weight sample of an ion exchanger was preliminarily held for 12 h in a model solution without Cs to establish the equilibrium at a volume/weight ratio of 1000 mL/g.
steps.searchable_summary: equilibrate ion exchanger in model solution

steps.description: The solution was replaced by a new one spiked with Cs-137 or containing a stable Cs-133 isotope.
steps.searchable_summary: replace solution with Cs-137 spiked solution

steps.description: To determine the Cs-137 distribution coefficient, the time of RFR's contact with the model solution was at least 24 h.
The solution was separated from RFR by filtering on an ash-free blue-ribbon filter, and the residual content of the Cs-137 radionuclide was determined.
steps.searchable_summary: filter solution and determine residual Cs-137

steps.description: The efficiency of the Cs-137 removal (%) from the model solution was calculated using a specific formula.
The kinetic parameters were evaluated at temperatures of 30, 50, and 70 °C under constant stirring using a thermostated shaker.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate kinetic parameters at various temperatures

steps.description: The experimental values were fitted using kinetics equations of the pseudo-first and pseudo-second orders.
steps.searchable_summary: fit experimental values to kinetics equations

steps.description: The completeness of Cs desorption in dependence on the feed rate of the eluent was evaluated under dynamic conditions.
